title: Suicide rate in 1980 vs. 2023
subtitle:
Estimated aumber of suicides per 100,000 people in a given population and year. Suicide deaths are underreported in
many countries due to social stigma and cultural or legal concerns. This data is based on modeling of global suicide
patterns, and includes adjustments that aim to account for missing data and underreporting.
note: To allow for comparisons between countries and over time, this metric is [age-standardized](#dod:age_standardized).
caption:
Suicide rate in 1980 vs. 2023

Estimated aumber of suicides per 100,000 people in a given population and year. Suicide deaths are underreported in
many countries due to social stigma and cultural or legal concerns. This data is based on modeling of global suicide
patterns, and includes adjustments that aim to account for missing data and underreporting.

This scatter plot compares age-standardized suicide rates per 100,000 people in 1980 against rates in 2023 for various countries. The horizontal axis represents the 2023 rate, ranging from 0 to over 25, while the vertical axis represents the 1980 rate, ranging from 0 to 35. Each point is colored by continent and sized by population. Russia and Lithuania sit in the upper right quadrant, with 2023 rates near 20 and 1980 rates exceeding 30. Hungary and Slovenia also appear high on the vertical axis, with 1980 rates above 30 and 32 respectively, while their 2023 rates sit between 10 and 15. China is represented by a large teal circle positioned at a 2023 rate of approximately 5 and a 1980 rate near 22. India appears as a large purple circle with a 2023 rate around 13 and a 1980 rate near 10. Guyana sits at the far right of the chart with a 2023 rate exceeding 25 and a 1980 rate near 23. Lesotho and Eritrea show 2023 rates between 20 and 25 with 1980 rates around 22. In contrast, countries like Qatar, Indonesia, and Bangladesh cluster in the bottom left corner, with both 1980 and 2023 rates falling below 5. Many European nations, including France, Germany, and Austria, cluster in the middle of the chart with 1980 rates between 15 and 25 and 2023 rates between 10 and 15. The distribution shows a general trend where countries with higher rates in 1980 often maintain higher relative positions in 2023, though specific values vary across the plotted points.
